title: Coins, Stamps, Autographs, Sports Cards
description:
175 lots of Coins, Sports Cards, Autographs, and Stamps. 

This exciting auction features a spectacular cross-category collection spanning **coins and currency, sports cards, celebrity autographs, and stamps/postal history** with great finds for numismatists, sports card collectors, autograph hounds, and philatelists alike. Headlining this sale are **six PCGS/NGC-certified U.S. $20 Gold Double Eagles** (1904, 1911D, 1924, and 1928 dates graded MS62–MS63), anchoring a deep coin lineup that includes over a dozen lots of **Morgan Silver Dollars** (1880–1921), **Peace Silver Dollars**, full and partial rolls of **pre-1965 silver quarters, dimes, and half dollars**, **Indian Head pennies** dating to 1865, **Buffalo Nickels**, a 1929 Brown Seal $20 Boston bank note, Series 1953B Red Seal $2 bills, 1976 sequential $2 bills, US Mint and Proof sets, Scottsdale .999 silver bullion, and a **Lincoln Cent book that includes the coveted 1909 VDB penny**. 

Hockey collectors and Detroit Red Wings fans will be battling over a **massive Steve Yzerman / Detroit Red Wings PSA-graded collection** — easily one of the most comprehensive Yzerman lots we've ever offered. The crown jewel is a **1984 Topps #49 Steve Yzerman Rookie graded PSA 10 Gem Mint with a population of only 80**, alongside a PSA 9 of the same card, a **1984 O-Pee-Chee #67 Rookie PSA 9**, a **1984 7-Eleven Disc PSA 10 Gem Mint**, a **1993 Topps Premier PSA 10 (Pop 23)**, and the scarce **1985 Topps #29 PSA 9 (Pop 140)** and **1986 Topps #11 PSA 9 (Pop 38)** — plus dozens more PSA 9 and PSA 10 Yzerman cards spanning his entire career. Red Wings memorabilia lots with plaques, McFarlane figures, bobbleheads, and a **Niklas Lidstrom graded card and collectibles lot** round out the Hockeytown offerings. 

Sports card collectors will also find **1971 Topps Baseball lots featuring Johnny Bench #250**, high-graded baseball cards of Verlander, Bob Feller, Christian Yelich, Mark McGwire, and Corey Seager, a **Justin Jefferson 2021 Panini Rookie D-9**, football card lots loaded with Jerry Rice, Dan Marino, Randy Moss, and Brett Favre, plus basketball cards featuring Patrick Ewing, Chris Paul, and Paul George. 

Autograph highlights include **Audrey Hepburn signed "Moon River" sheet music** from *Breakfast at Tiffany's*, a **John Wayne signed lobby card** from *The Shootist*, a **James Stewart signed lobby card** from *Mr. Hobbs Takes a Vacation*, **Carrie Fisher and Mark Hamill signed Star Wars 8x10 photos**, a **Steven Spielberg signed *Close Encounters* photo**, **Robert De Niro signed *Raging Bull* photo**, a **Vincent Price signed 11x15**, **Ozzy Osbourne signed *Diary of a Madman* LP jacket**, **Dolly Parton signed photos**, and **two signed photos from Claire Danes and Leonardo DiCaprio**. Political history collectors will prize autographs from **British PM Harold Wilson, Israeli PM Golda Meir, and German Chancellor Willy Brandt**, along with a **Sir Edmund Hillary signed letterhead** from the first man to make it to the top of Everest.

 Additional autograph lots include horror icons **Ingrid Pitt, Linda Blair, and PJ Soles**, Hollywood legends **Liza Minnelli, Debbie Reynolds, Doris Day, and Jessica Tandy**, comedy greats **Chevy Chase, Jerry Lewis, and Albert Brooks**, TV favorites **Henry Winkler, Mayim Bialik, and Rainn Wilson**, sports legends **Magic Johnson, Franco Harris, 
Terry Bradshaw, Jim Kelly, Joe Sakic, and Stan Mikita**, a huge **country music autograph lot** featuring Ernest Tubb and Stonewall Jackson, rock and pop signed pieces from **Lou Reed, Dee Snider, Chad Smith, and Donna Summer**, plus a **Richard Corben autographed art book with CoA**, **Walter Lantz signed 8x10**, **Forrest J. Ackerman signed card**, **David Cronenberg signed book plate**, **Ramsey Lewis signed LP**, and a **1966 Chad & Jeremy fan club lot with signed photo**. 

Stamp and postal history enthusiasts will find **five lots of 100-count 19th-century postal history covers (1879–1900s)** with special and blue cancellations, **1916 advertising covers**, **a rare 1964 JFK German First Day Cover**, **1901 Pan-American Exposition Niagara Falls stamp (Scott #297)**, **Sanda Island gold foil JFK stamps**, **Tristan da Cunha Princess Diana stamp sheets**, **Argentina full sheets** (1913, 1934, and 500 Pesos Orchid), multiple lots of rare **1920s stock transfer stamps**, and assorted international stamps from Vatican City, France, Uganda, Scotland, Turkey, and more.

For local bidders, lots from this auction may be picked up and invoices can be paid starting at 12pm noon on the Monday following the auction's close. For everyone else, shipping costs will be assessed and invoices will start becoming available later that day. Pick-up is available Monday through Friday, 9 AM to 5 PM, at our shipping & receiving entrance.
Please note we have moved locations so make sure you are picking up at the Farmington Hills location!

2026 Jared Hammell Memorial Volleyball Tournament Silent Auction

This auction supports the 25th Annual Jared Hammell Memorial Volleyball Tournament. This tournament is held in honor of Chatfield FFA member Jared Hammell, who passed away too soon and is one of Chatfield FFA's most successful events of the year.

Saturday, March 14th 2026 at 11am EDT. (March 2026 Session 1 of 2)
The March 14th & 15th 2026 Wartime Military Memorabilia Auction is an exceptional 2 session (815 lot) militaria collector sale, including a broad offering of militaria from the Civil War, Indian Wars, Spanish-American War, World War I / WWI, World War II / WWII, Korean War, Vietnam War, Gulf Wars & Current Conflicts.
The auction brings to market a significant offering of collectible militaria, including named groupings / archives, uniforms, orders & medals, awards, patches, insignia, flags & cloth, headgear, field gear, personal effects, ordnance, paper ephemera & letters, photographs, posters, art, equipment, trench art, books, and optics.
Session 1 (413 Lots) | Saturday, March 14th 2026 at 11am EDT
Uniforms, Named Groupings, Cloth & Flags, Patches, Insignia, Orders & Medals, Personal Effects, & Ordnance
Session 2 (405 Lots) | Sunday, March 15th 2026 at 1pm EDT
Headgear, Field Gear, Trench Art, Posters, Images & Art, Models, Books, Letters, & Paper Ephemera
Each Wartime Military Memorabilia Auction is comprised of militaria collections consigned to sell from military museum deaccessions, individuals, military dealer inventory, lifelong wartime military memorabilia collections, and estate / trust wartime military memorabilia collections across the United States.
Considering selling a wartime military memorabilia collection? Speak with us today. Our comprehensive collectible wartime militaria auction marketing platform is a powerful conduit to ensure your collectible military pieces reach millions of serious collectors around the globe.
We travel nationally to meet with clients, consult on collections, and to pick up & transport collections.
As an alternative to consignment, we also purchase collections (receive immediate payment).

title: Lammers Estate Auction of Knoxville, TN RD 2
description:
Welcome to Round 2 of the Lammers Estate Auction.
      The Lammers family proudly served our country in the United States Navy. After retiring as a Commander  from the naval base in Portsmouth, Lennis Lammer moved again and the family eventually made their home in Knoxville, where they became cherished members of the community.This second round continues to showcase the remarkable life and collections of this distinguished family
  .Mrs. Bea Lammers, a direct descendant of Chief George Skye, was a passionate and knowledgeable collector of authentic Native American artifacts. Her collection features carefully curated pottery, beadwork, weavings, sculptures, and cultural pieces — thoughtfully gathered over a lifetime, with an emphasis on quality and authenticity rather than tourist ware.Their son, 
     Steven, also served in the military, and this round includes treasured memorabilia, keepsakes, and meaningful pieces the family carried with them during transfers between naval bases — each item holding a story of service and family tradition.
    Daughter Laura, a model, author, and beloved member of the Knoxville community, devotedly cared for her parents. Her presence is reflected in elegant vintage handbags and clothing from her modeling career, as well as her beautifully displayed miniature collections — each piece a testament to her artistic eye and love of detail.As we continue uncovering and organizing the estate, future rounds will reveal even more extraordinary finds.Thank you for your continued interest and support.For questions, please contact Jolene at 865-601-1371.

title: 03-15-2026 Important Fine Art Auction
description:
Important Fine Art Auction!

Online Auction  7PM Sunday, March 15, 2026
Bid Now! www.tomhallauctions.com

This curated online auction presents an outstanding collection of fine art that spans mediums,
movements, and generations of artistic mastery. The offering includes oil, acrylic, and watercolor
paintings alongside sculptures, expressive sketches, pencil and charcoal works, pastels, and
richly layered mixed media pieces. Both framed and unframed works are available, allowing
collectors flexibility in presentation and investment. Styles range from Impressionistic and
abstract compositions to realism, still life, landscape, and beyond, creating a dynamic and
visually compelling catalog.

Highlights include works by William Hugh Ferguson, celebrated for his refined academic
technique and sensitive portraiture, with a special emphasis on his beautifully executed
sketches that reveal his mastery of form, light, and human expression. These works on paper
offer a rare glimpse into Ferguson’s creative process, capturing movement and emotion with
remarkable economy and precision. The auction also features Western watercolor paintings by
Allen M. Dean, known for his atmospheric depictions of frontier life and expansive landscapes
that embody the spirit of the American West.

Sculptural works by internationally recognized artist Steve Tobin bring bold organic forms and
powerful presence, while pieces by Aldro T. Hibbard reflect the rich tradition of American
Impressionism with luminous color and dynamic brushwork. Works by Zvi Mairovich offer
striking contemporary abstraction with energetic texture and movement, complemented by the
refined modernist sensibilities of Klaus Ihlenfeld. The collection further includes compelling
works by Stephen George Maniatty, Hans Moller, and noted American painter William Langson
Lathrop, whose landscapes helped shape early 20th-century Impressionism in the United
States.

Rounding out the auction is a finely detailed KPM hand-painted portrait plaque, representing the
renowned German porcelain tradition prized for its craftsmanship and artistic excellence.
Together, this diverse and thoughtfully assembled collection offers something for every collector,
from museum-quality names to expressive works full of character and history. Whether you are
seeking a timeless classic, a striking contemporary piece, or rare artist studies, this auction
provides an exceptional opportunity to acquire beautiful and meaningful art.

Aldro Thompson Hibbard (American, 1886–1972)
Prominent Rockport School painter celebrated for bold impasto New England winter landscapes and one of the leading Massachusetts Impressionists.

Steve Tobin (American, b. 1957)
Internationally recognized contemporary sculptor known for monumental bronze, glass, and steel works exploring nature and growth forms, including his well-known Root series exhibited in museums and public spaces worldwide.

William Langson Lathrop (American, 1859–1938)
Important founding member of the New Hope Art Colony; American Impressionist known for atmospheric Pennsylvania river landscapes and seasonal rural views.

Hans Moller (American, 1857–1925)
German-born American painter active in Philadelphia and New Hope circles, recognized for Tonalist landscapes and coastal scenes with moody, subdued palettes.

William H. Ferguson (American, 1850–1923) (over 100 pieces from this artist)
Pennsylvania Impressionist-associated landscape painter active in Philadelphia, noted for atmospheric rural and river scenes influenced by late-19th-century American tonalism.

Klaus Ihlenfeld (German-American, 1934–2005)
Sculptor and designer known for cast-bronze furniture and sculptural forms built from dense, organic clusters resembling reeds and natural growth. Active in the United States in the late 20th century, his work blends modernist design with fine art and is collected as both decorative art and sculpture.

Plus many additional listed and unlisted artists – original oils, lithographs, and framed works – offering collecting opportunities in every price range.

Preview by appointment, call 610-799-0808

Items are located at Tom Hall Auction Gallery, 4644 Rt. 309, Schnecksville, Pa. 18078

Pick up hours at the gallery following the auction:  12noon-3:30pm the day after the auction and Monday thru Friday from 9:30AM-3:30PM  daily.

All items MUST be removed within 7 DAYS of the auction.

Local deliver available. Please call us at 610-799-0808 for information.

Online Only Bidding: Auction starts closing with 2 items closing per minute, bidding continues as auction closes.Extended bidding, if a bid is placed within the last 1 minute of a lot’s scheduled close time the bidding will be extended 1 minute from the last bid.

Buying at auction is not like buying new; the items are used, vintage and antique. They are consigned by estates and people downsizing. They have been used in people’s homes for their purposes, so while most items are in good condition, most are not in perfect condition. We provide private previews for the public to come to the gallery and physically inspect items being sold. If you cannot be here for the inspection, please contact us by email for additional photos or more detailed condition reports.

We try to present items to bidders fairly; however, it is the bidder’s duty to make independent investigation of the condition of items before bidding.

Online bidding Terms:  Major Credit Cards, 20% Buyer’s Premium, 6% Sales Tax collected if you have provided a valid Sales Tax Exemption Certificate. 

IMPORTANT PLEASE READ PAYMENT TERMS FOR THIS AUCTION 

All major credit cards are accepted. Your credit  card will immediatley be charged for all purchases. 

However at auctioneers discretion INVOICES EXCEEDING $1200 may required payment by wire transfer or cash. 

Do You Have Something to Sell? We Can Help!
Real Estate Sales, Downsizing & Estate Services
Business Liquidations, Jewelry, Couture, Motorcycles & Car Collections
Estate Appraisals
Tom Hall Auctions 610-799-0808

Ben Ladage Auctions - Auburn, IL
Office: 217-652-0924
View full catalog and bid at: www.benladage.com
Please read the complete Terms & Conditions before placing any bids.
________________________________________
BUYER'S PREMIUM & PAYMENT:

A 15% buyer's premium will be added to each item.
We offer a 3% discount for cash, check, or non-credit card payment - you must notify us before the auction closes to receive this discount.

Accepted payment forms: Visa, Mastercard, Discover, debit card, cash, or approved checks.
No checks accepted without prior approval or established business history. Checks with bank letter of credit may be accepted-please call our office in advance.

Invoices for items will be billed to your registered credit card immediately following the auction. If shipping is selected, you will receive a separate invoice once shipping costs are calculated.
________________________________________
FOLLOWING THE AUCTION:

Winning bidders will receive both an email and text message with the option to select pickup or shipping.
o If pickup is selected, you'll receive full pickup instructions, including date, time, and location based on the information you provided during registration.
o If shipping is selected, your items will be securely packed by our in-house shipping department. Once packaging is complete, you will receive another email and text message with the total shipping cost and handling fee.
________________________________________
SHIPPING:

Shipping is available on most items and provided in-house by Ben Ladage Auctions using Shipping Saint. After the auction:
"	You will receive an email and text to choose either shipping or pick-up.
"	If shipping is selected, items will be packaged, and you will receive a second notification with shipping and handling costs.
"	We use USPS, UPS, and FedEx and always select the most cost-effective method.
"	Please allow up to 15 business days for items to ship after the pickup date, although most packages go out within 5 business days.
We do not ship large or heavy items unless pre-approved prior to auction closing. We do not accept outside shipping labels or provide shipping quotes. Shipping costs are the responsibility of the buyer and are non-refundable.
________________________________________
ITEM DESCRIPTIONS, INSPECTIONS, & DISCLAIMERS:

All items are sold "AS IS, WHERE IS" with no warranties, guarantees, refunds, or returns of any kind. Descriptions including condition, age, origin, or rarity are offered as opinion only. The bidder assumes all responsibility for determining value and authenticity.
Preview and inspection are strongly encouraged. The auctioneer is not responsible for errors in descriptions or missed bids (online, absentee, phone, or otherwise). All announcements on auction day take precedence over any prior marketing.
________________________________________
PICKUP POLICY - READ CAREFULLY:

All items must be picked up on the scheduled date and time. If you cannot make the scheduled pickup, please do not bid.
Items not picked up are forfeited and become property of Ben Ladage Auctions unless other arrangements have been made in advance.
We understand life happens and will try to work with you-but communication is key. If we have to retrieve your items from the auction site and transport them back to Auburn, a minimum fee of $25 per item will be charged. This fee helps cover time, labor, and tracking logistics.
________________________________________
ABSENTEE BIDDING & TECHNICAL INFORMATION:

Don't want to watch the auction live? The best strategy is to leave your maximum bid ahead of time. Our system will bid incrementally on your behalf.
This is a soft close auction. If a bid is placed in the last 3 minutes of a lot's closing, that lot will automatically extend by 3 minutes. All lots close in staggered intervals.
If you're unsure how to bid online, contact our office before the final day-we're happy to help! Absentee bids are accepted and will include the 15% buyer's premium.
________________________________________

Sellers: Shirley Antonacci 

We appreciate your bids and support-thank you for participating in this online auction!

title: Militaria - Headgear, Field Gear, Posters, Paper Ephemera
description:
Wartime Military Memorabilia Auction - Civil War, Indian Wars, Spanish-American War, World War 1 / WWI, World War II / WWII, Korean War, Vietnam War, Cold War, & Modern Conflicts | Headgear, Field Gear, Trench Art, Posters, Images & Art, Models, Books, Letters, & Paper Ephemera. Live Webcast: Sunday, March 15th 2026 at 1pm EDT (March 2026 Session 2 of 2)
The March 14th & 15th 2026 Wartime Military Memorabilia Auction is an exceptional 2 session (815 lot) militaria collector sale, including a broad offering of militaria from the Civil War, Indian Wars, Spanish-American War, World War I / WWI, World War II / WWII, Korean War, Vietnam War, Gulf Wars & Current Conflicts.
The auction brings to market a significant offering of collectible militaria, including named groupings / archives, uniforms, orders & medals, awards, patches, insignia, flags & cloth, headgear, field gear, personal effects, ordnance, paper ephemera & letters, photographs, posters, art, equipment, trench art, books, and optics.
Session 2 (405 Lots) | Sunday, March 15th 2026 at 1pm EDT
Headgear, Field Gear, Trench Art, Posters, Images & Art, Models, Books, Letters, & Paper Ephemera
Session 1 (413 Lots) | Saturday, March 14th 2026 at 11am EDT
Uniforms, Named Groupings, Cloth & Flags, Patches, Insignia, Orders & Medals, Personal Effects, & Ordnance
Each Wartime Military Memorabilia Auction is comprised of militaria collections consigned to sell from military museum deaccessions, individuals, military dealer inventory, lifelong wartime military memorabilia collections, and estate / trust wartime military memorabilia collections across the United States.

treasures gathered from a variety of refined estates. The sale features an eclectic yet
sophisticated array of items including fine pottery, sculptures, artwork, artisan duck decoys,
horse-themed collectibles, clocks, trinkets, vintage teddy bears, and elegant vintage Saks Fifth
Avenue fashions. Collectors will find pieces crafted from porcelain, glass, bronze, wood, and
textiles, ranging in style from refined European elegance to rustic hand-carved artistry.
Featured makers and brands include Herend, Tiffany, Hermes, Royal Dux, Franklin Mint, Alka
Kunst, and other respected names in fine decorative arts. Among the highlights is a striking L.
C. Tiffany Favrile glass vase measuring 7.25 inches tall, displaying a flared rim and a vibrant
iridescent finish in hues of pink, gold, and purple, enhanced by organic drip designs that
accentuate its luminous surface. The underside bears etched markings characteristic of Tiffany
Studios’ craftsmanship. Also of note is a Tiffany & Co. bronze Aladdin’s lamp floor lamp, an
antique gilt bronze Victorian jewelry casket, the elegant Charles Bell Birch “Wood Nymph”
Parianware figure, and a beautifully framed Hermes “Le Monde du Polo” scarf measuring 35
inches by 35 inches.
From delicate porcelain and shimmering glass to velvet fashions, rustic carved sculptures, and
unique decorative objects, this auction offers a wide range of distinctive pieces that will appeal
to collectors and decorators alike. With far too many wonderful items to list, bidders are
encouraged to explore the catalog and discover the remarkable variety within this upscale
collection.

Preview the auction by appointment, call 610-799-0808
Items are located at: Tom Hall Auction Gallery, 4644 Main St (Rte 309),Schnecksville, Pa. 18078

Buyer Pick up times after the auction: 12PM-3:30PM the day after the auction,  9:30AM-3:30PM Monday – Friday  within 7 days of the auction.

In-house shipping!  
Bid confidently as we handle the packing and shipping straight from our gallery to you. 

Consider using our professional in-house shipping department for your auction treasures.

With over 30 years of packaging and shipping experience our in-house shipping department has you covered.

For shipping inquiries, contact us at: shipping@tomhallauctions.com.

Online bidding Terms: Major Credit Cards, 20% Buyer’s  for this auction, 6% Sales Tax collected if you have not provided a valid Sales Tax Exemption Certificate.

Payment is by major credit card. Checks or pay pal are not accepted.

(IMPORTANT NOTE: At auctioneers discretion, payment may be by wire transfer or cash for all purchases with an invoice exceeding $1200.00.)  Auctioneer may require a copy of valid ID for any transactions.

title: JLA Showroom-Spring Break Steals Auction
description:
Get ready for an incredible auction packed with treasures for every collector and home! 

Elegant Crystal:
Discover dazzling pieces from renowned makers including Lalique, Baccarat, Waterford, Steuben, Fenton, Fostoria, and Blenko, perfect for collectors or anyone who loves timeless sparkle.

Fine China & Pottery:
Beautiful and highly collectible selections featuring Royal Crown Derby, MacKenzie-Childs, Belleek, Royal Worcester (including Dorothy Doughty birds), Lenox, Wedgwood, Roseville, Flow Blue, and Limoges.

Silver & Decorative Metals:
A wonderful assortment of sterling silver, silverplate, and classic aluminum wares ready to grace your table or collection.

Jewelry Treasures:
From stylish costume jewelry to elegant silver pieces and even loose gemstones, there's something to catch every eye. An added treat for Easter - we offer beautiful enameled egg pendants and earrings!

Unique Collectibles:
Perfumes, stunning art glass, menorahs, and fun man-cave beer sign collectibles that will add character to any space.

Quality Furniture:
Featuring a King Hickory sofa and chair, Windsor bench, bookcase secretary, side tables, Regency and Mid-Century Modern chairs, and a charming lingerie chest.

Lighting Galore:
A large selection of beautiful table lamps and dramatic floor torchieres to brighten any room.

Artwork:
An impressive variety including vintage botanical prints, Thai temple rubbings, and Texas wildflower art. 

Plus, So Much More:
A classic Featherweight Singer sewing machine, a Stihl trimmer, several rugs, and plenty of surprises throughout the sale.

Whether you're a collector, decorator, or bargain hunter, this auction truly has something for everyone!

title: UsedTwo Auction 81
description:
Welcome to UsedTwo Auctions. Preview images of catalog items will become available as upcoming auctions are processed. The complete Auction Catalog, including all preview images, will be available by Monday evening prior to the commencement of the auction.

In-person preview appointments are available Tuesday through Thursday from 10:00 a.m. to 2:00 p.m. during the active auction period. Previews will be conducted at our Frederick warehouse, situated at 7313 Grove Road, Warehouse Unit G, Frederick, MD 21704. Please contact 301-685-5677 to confirm appointment availability.

Auctions operate from Tuesday to Tuesday. Item collection is scheduled for the Thursday following auction closure, from 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM. In the event that Thursday pickup is not feasible, please contact us via email at team@usedtwo.com prior to auction close to arrange an alternative pickup time. Items remaining uncollected by the close of business on the following Thursday, for which no rescheduling arrangement has been communicated, may be forfeited. UsedTwo disclaims liability for any items not retrieved within the designated pickup timeframe.

Shipping/Pickup Information

We do not provide shipping services. However, clients may utilize our local UPS shipping service for item pickup, packing, and shipping.

Important Notice:

Items must be scheduled for pickup no later than 9 AM on the Friday following the auction's conclusion. UPS will collect items on the Friday following the auction's close, provided all required documentation has been submitted.

Documentation Requirements:
A photograph of the signed invoice.
A photograph of the driver's license with matching address.
Both documents must be emailed prior to the release of items for shipping to team@usedtwo.com and The UPS Store at store5681@theupsstore.com. Failure to provide the required documentation will result in the items not being released, invoices not being refunded, and forfeiture of the items. Packing services are provided exclusively by UPS. Clients are required to utilize our designated local UPS Store for pickup, packing, and shipping services. Prepaid shipping labels are not accepted. A copy of the invoice and a shipping address matching the address on file with Hibid must be submitted to The UPS Store before items can be released for shipping and packing. For out-of-state clients and those purchasing jewelry, gold, silver, or other items at our discretion, additional requirements apply: 1. A copy of the invoice and a shipping address matching the Hibid address must be submitted to The UPS Store. 2. A valid driver's license (photo or scan) with an address matching the Hibid address must be provided. 3. The paid invoice (attached to the winning bidder email) must be signed, photographed/scanned, and emailed back to us. Release of items to UPS pickup is contingent upon receipt of all required documents.

To schedule a pickup time and date, please email team@usedtwo.com, specifying that items should be collected from UsedTwo Auctions, 7313 Grove Road Unit G, Frederick MD 21704. Contact information for The UPS Store is as follows:

Your Team at The UPS Store
5257 Buckeystown Pike
Frederick, MD 21704
Phone: 301-620-8825 Fax: 301-620-8821
Website: https://www.theupsstore.com/5681
Email: store5681@theupsstore.com

Subscribe to our Newsletter and follow us on Facebook to become a UsedTwo Auction Insider, gaining access to pre-auction Sneak Peeks and more.

Please ensure UsedTwo communications are not directed to your spam folder. We aim to promptly notify you of winning bids.

For any questions or concerns, please email team@usedtwo.com.

title: Coin & Currency Auction – Paper Money,  CC Morgans, Key Date
description:
Becker Rare Coin & Currency Auction

Carson City Silver Dollars o Key Date Coins o Historic U.S. Currency o Proof Sets

Starts Closing Wednesday, March 18th @ 7:00 PM

This outstanding auction features a large selection of collectible U.S. coins and historic currency, including several key dates and certified examples. Highlights include a 1899 Black Eagle $1 Silver Certificate Star Note, 1928 $20 Gold Certificate, and Civil War Confederate currency. Coin collectors will find an impressive lineup of Carson City Morgan Silver Dollars including GSA Hoard examples, a rare 1880-CC 8/7 overdate, 1909-S VDB Lincoln cent (PCGS), 1927 Peace Dollar, and numerous certified Morgan and Peace Silver Dollars graded up to MS65.

Additional highlights include a 2016-W Mercury Dime Centennial Gold Coin, Proof American Silver Eagles, rolls of 90% silver Mercury dimes, and an extensive run of U.S. Mint proof and uncirculated sets spanning multiple decades.

Whether you're a collector, investor, or simply enjoy historic American coinage, this auction offers many desirable pieces.

title: 1172 Mount Joy Rd. Manheim, PA 17545
description: This Crossroads Community–zoned property offers an excellent opportunity for business owners, contractors, or investors seeking a well-located and functional property. Located between Manheim and Mount Joy, this former small excavation company property features strong road frontage, good visibility, and convenient access to Routes 283, 72, and 76. The separate office building is well suited for business operations and includes three private offices, two reception areas, a bathroom, and a garage space with an overhead door. A dedicated parking lot provides ample off-street parking for employees, customers, and business vehicles. A large barn with an overhead garage door offers additional space for equipment storage, workshop use, or other business needs. The property also includes a 4+ bedroom home, allowing for on-site living or potential rental income. The first floor features an open kitchen and dining area, family room, office, powder room, laundry room, and bonus room. The second floor includes a primary bedroom, three additional bedrooms, and a full bathroom with an unfinished basement providing additional storage. With flexible zoning, existing office and shop buildings, ample parking, and a highly accessible location, this property is well positioned for permitted business uses as well as residential occupancy, subject to township regulations.

title: March Sportsman's Auction
description:
March Sportsman's Auction – Quality. Collectible. Ready to Bid.
Join us for our upcoming March Firearms Auction, featuring a strong lineup of rifles, shotguns, and handguns from trusted makers and respected collections. Whether you’re a seasoned collector, sporting enthusiast, or first-time buyer, this sale offers something for every level of interest.

This auction includes
:• Modern and vintage firearms
• Sporting rifles and shotguns
• Revolvers and semi-automatic pistols
• Collector pieces
• Ammunition and related accessories (where applicable)

All firearms will be sold in accordance with federal and state laws. Required background checks will be conducted, and transfers will be handled through a licensed FFL dealer. Please review all terms and conditions prior to bidding.Bid with confidence. Bid competitively. Bid responsibly.If we have it — you just might need it.

title: All-Brick Home in Seymour, TN Subject to Court Confirmation
description:
Welcome to this well-maintained all-brick single-family home located the desirable Boon Docks subdivision in Seymour, TN. This spacious single-level rancher offers 3 bedrooms and 2 full baths, providing comfortable and functional living all on one level.

Situated on a generous 0.62-acre lot, the home features a 2-car garage, a large, detached shed for extra storage or hobbies, and multiple outdoor living spaces. Relax year-round in the sunroom or entertain guests on the outdoor patio area complete with an arbor. Inside, you’ll find a cozy fireplace that anchors the living space and adds warmth and character.

With its peaceful setting, ample outdoor space, and desirable location, this home offers the perfect blend of comfort, convenience, and charm.

349 Fallen Oak Circle, Seymour, TN 37865

title: 140 W. Reynolds Rd. New Oxford, PA 17350
description: This picturesque property is truly vacation-destination worthy and an opportunity you won’t want to miss! Spanning over 78 acres of farmland, woodlands, and open spaces, it features a spectacular all-stone and brick four-bedroom, four-bathroom home, just 20 minutes from historic Gettysburg. The custom home welcomes you with a formal entryway and continues with a formal dining room with a crystal chandelier. The kitchen boasts custom cabinetry by Dovetail Woodworks and a cozy breakfast nook, flowing into a sunroom with outdoor access, a full bathroom, and a family room with built-in bookcases by Dovetail Woodworks and a stone floor to ceiling fireplace. The main level also includes a bedroom currently used as an office & two master suites—each with two closets and a full bathroom, one of which features custom cabinetry from Dovetail Woodworks—plus a screened-in porch & laundry room. Upstairs, you will find a fourth bedroom along with a full bathroom and a large storage area. The full basement is unfinished and features high ceilings, offering the potential to create an additional 2,500 sq. ft. of finished living space. Beautiful oak floors from the property run throughout the main level, while wide-plank pine floors from the barn extend across the second floor, complemented by oak doors and poplar trim, as well as 9-foot ceilings throughout the home, cathedral ceilings in the family room and sunroom, and tray ceilings in one of the master bedrooms and the formal dining room. Outside, the property offers a half-acre pond stocked with bluegill, perch, and bass, with electricity nearby, a spacious two story bank barn, a large three bay pole building with a finished shop section, an outdoor fire pit area w/ storage surrounded by stone pillars and custom railing, and outdoor patio along with diverse wildlife and three deer stands. Currently, 60 acres of farmland are leased, and the woodlands were timbered approximately 15 years ago. Whether you’re looking for your next family home, a country retreat, or a private getaway, this property delivers it all!

title: March Exceptional Estates Auction
description:
FINE ART
This auction includes work by dozens of important, listed artists. Antique paintings, modern and contemporary paintings, prints and sculpture. Pablo Picasso, Charles Wysocki, Rembrandt, Richard MacDonald, Gene Davis, Peter Max, Harold Kraus, Antoine Falardeau, Leonardo Nierman, E.K.K. Wetherill, Joseph Sheppard, Douglas Hofman, Otto Piltz, Armando Amaya and many others, including one of only five six-foot-tall “Golden Trump” bronze statues by Tommy Zegan.


JEWELRY
A massive selection of outstanding jewelry. Tiffany & Co., Roberto Coin, and other designers. From antique to modern, there’s something for every taste: bracelets, rings, necklaces and pendants, and watches.


U.S. GOLD COINS
Dozens of PCGS and NGC graded gold coins, many scarce and rare dates..


STERLING SILVER
Several outstanding sets of sterling silver flatware, as well as serving pieces. Highlights include a rare Georg Jensen Pyramid serving tray and a Georg Jensen coffee pot. 


ANTIQUE & MODERN FIREARMS
An interesting selection of pistols and long guns, from percussion to contemporary models. Some very rare examples, including a Colt Stalker, one of 200 ever made. More Colts are in the auction, as well as Browning, Luger, Walther, Steyr, AR platform black rifles, and more.


MUCH MORE, AS USUAL
Document signed by John Quincy Adams as president, Chinese art and antiques, scarce salt and pepper shakers, a signed first edition of Gone With the Wind, an old and well-documented collection of Native American points, etc.


Do not miss browsing the catalog.


PLEASE NOTE OUR TERMS REGARDING INVOICES, PAYMENTS, AND SHIPPING. When you register to bid, you agree to our terms. 
Online bidding available through BidSpirit, our app, Bidsquare, Live Auctioneers, Invaluable, and HiBid. Phone bids (greater of $100 or posted opening bid) and absentee bids accepted -- form is on our website.*Our in-house buyer premium is 17% and applies to bids placed in person, absentee, by phone. Our online buyer premium is 20% onBidSpirit, our app, HiBid, and BidSquare. 22% on Live Auctioneers, and Invaluable/AuctionZip. Not local? No problem – we can ship some items worldwide and provide you with a list of shippers for the other items.

title: Tommy Shomo Estate on Hampden Sydney Campus
description:
We are proud to offer the sale of items from the estate of Hampden Sydney's beloved assistant Dean of Students and a man who so much more to the campus, the late Tommy Shomo (Class of '69). 

The estate is full of unique items from Mr. Shomo's collection including a vast library of interesting books (many stamped with his library seal), nicely framed art, Asian antiques, bone china, American antiques, Biggs and Virginia Craftsmen furniture, Chippendale, Federal, Hipplewhite, and Queen Anne style furniture, outdoor furniture and items from his beautiful and cherished garden, a 2010 Ford Ranger pickup truck, sterling silver items, housewares, Persian rugs, tools, and so much more. 

More than that, there are items to remember Mr. Shomo by like teacups, champagne flutes, and other items that you might have seen at his famous etiquette dinners. As well as tools you may have seem him using while keeping his perfect little side-yard garden. 

Bidding on this estate will open when the photgraphy is complete and close beginning at 6:00PM Saturday, March 21st. All items must be picked up Sunday, March 22nd between 12:00 and 7:00 PM.

title: Two Prominant Estates
description:
Thank you for registering for our auction!  

Two prominant Estate Auctions
Caroline Ireland Estate in Lynn Haven, FL. and a $1.7 million dollar home (sold) in Panama City Beach, FL.

Ireland Estate Auction   Lynn Haven, Florida   2/22/2026-3/22/2026
This auction features a carefully curated collection of Antiques, Sculpture, Art, Gold, Sterling Silver, one of a kind Artisan Pottery pieces and much more from the Estate of Caroline Ireland.
Mrs. Ireland was known for her exquisite taste in fine art, including sculpture, artisan pottery, functional art, rugs and antiques. More importantly she was nationally known for her philanthropic activaties, President of the Caroline P. and Charles W Ireland Foundation, Her community service includes leadership positions that often focus on Education and the Arts serving on: The Board of Trustees for Whittier College, 1996 to 2012. The Lynn Haven Public Library Advisory Board, 1984-1987. The Board of the Panama City Episcopal Day School. Lynn Haven Public Building Board Chairman, 1970. Northwest Regional Library Board (Northwest Florida), 1963-1970. Founder of the Friends of the Bay County Public Libraries, Inc. Fund Raising Board Chairman for Lynn Haven Public Library Expansion. Building, 1981-1988. University of Alabama President's Council, 1970's. Member of the President's Council, University of Florida, 1989-1993 Advisory Board, New Orleans Museum of Art, 1991-1993. Founding member of the Sam P. Harn Museum, University of Florida, 1989. Member of the Advisory Board, Birmingham Museum of Art, Birmingham, Alabama, 1987 Chair of the Birmingham Museum of Art 30th Anniversary Ball, 1986. Member of the American Council for the Arts. Other service organizations she served and supported include: The Extension Board of the Chicago Maternity Center, 1960-1986 Board member of Protestant Baby Home, New Orleans, 1953 1954 Junior League of America chapters in New Orleans, Chicago and Birmingham. In 1984 Caroline and her late husband, Charles established the Caroline P. and Charles W. Ireland Endowment for Scholarly Distinction at the University of Alabama, Birmingham. The endowment supports two programs, The Caroline P. and Charles W. Ireland Award for Scholarly Distinction that recognizes the scholarly achievement in the arts and sciences by a current UAB faculty member and the Caroline P. and Charles W. Ireland. Distinguished Visiting Scholar Award that brings outstanding scholars who are generally recognized as figures of distinction in the arts and sciences to the UAB campus. In addition, the Caroline P. and Charles W. Ireland International Students Fund supports the university's international students. Most recently, she established the Caroline P. Ireland Endowed Award for Exceptional Students and Projects in Graduate Studies. This award fosters a commitment to research and discovery by providing up to ten scholarships annually to master degree students. Mrs. Ireland's support of UAB includes funding a conference room in the Reynolds Historical Library named in her honor, a major portion of the ninth floor in the West Pavilion, known as the Camellia Pavilion, as well as contributing to the Alys Robinson Stephens Performing Arts Center, the Department of Art and Art History, the School of Nursing and the Camellia Medical Group.

FOLLOWING THE AUCTION:

Winning bidders will receive both an email and text message with the option to select pickup or shipping.
o If pickup is selected, you'll receive full pickup instructions, including date, time, and location based on the information you provided during registration.
o If shipping is selected, your items will be securely packed by our in-house shipping department. Once packaging is complete, you will receive another email and text message with the total shipping cost and handling fee.

ABSENTEE BIDDING & TECHNICAL INFORMATION:

title: Sports Cards and Marvel Collectibles
description:
Auction Manager: Jensen Jeffrey
Contact Phone: 260-273-5931
This blockbuster auction is packed with premium sports cards, high-grade rookies, autographs, game-used memorabilia, and an extensive collection of Marvel & DC action figures, statues, and collectibles. Hundreds of lots featuring blue-chip graded cards, modern chase parallels, vintage classics, and superhero icons.
Major Categories & Highlights:
Modern Football & Basketball Rookies / Parallels:
2024 Topps Chrome Drake Maye 1974 X-Factor PSA 10
2024 Prizm TJ Watt Green Wave Gem Mint 10

Multiple 2024 Optic / Prizm / Absolute rookies (Xavier Worthy, Adonai Mitchell, Bo Nix, Jayden Daniels, JJ McCarthy)
Caitlin Clark rookies & custom Kaboom Cracked Ice cards
Tyrese Haliburton, Ja Morant, Anthony Edwards, Zion Williamson graded hits
Graded Vintage & Hall of Fame Stars

Shaquille O’Neal PSA 8–9 (Finest, Ultra, Stadium Club)
Peyton Manning multiple PGC 10s
LeBron James, Shohei Ohtani, Aaron Judge BMG 10s
Vintage standouts: 1955–56 Topps Al Kaline PSA, 1981 Topps Magic Johnson PSA 9, 1981 Topps Basketball Wax Pack PSA 6

Autographs, Relics & Game-Used:
SGC Authenticated Willie Stargell Autograph
Game-used jersey/net/patch fusions (Carmelo Anthony net, Marilyn Monroe personally used bed sheet, Elvis Presley jersey fusion, Fernando Tatis Jr., Barry Larkin, Tyrese Maxey)
Multiple Donruss Press Proof game-used footballs
Custom Kaboom Cracked Ice (Shohei Ohtani, Paige Bueckers, Caitlin Clark)

Marvel & DC Action Figures, Statues & Collectibles:
Extensive Marvel Legends series (multiple Silver Wolverine, Captain America, Iron Man, Thor, Hulk, Quicksilver, Storm, Scarlet Witch, Elektra, Daredevil)
Kotobukiya ArtFX statues (Batman, Black Widow)
DC Black & White statues (Batman, Joker by Jim Lee/Andy Kubert)
Justice League 10-inch figures (multiple assortments of Batman, Superman, Wonder Woman, Flash)
Large flats of HeroClix / WizKids figures
Busts & mini-busts (Ultimate Hulk, Thor, Captain America, Green Goblin)
Specialty items (Batman V Superman Ultimate Batcave playset, voice changer helmets, Thor hammer)
Other Notable Items:
Framed posters/prints (Marvel Avengers, Fenway Park)
Hot Wheels Monster Jam trucks, Simpsons figures, Star Wars, NASCAR diecast
Miscellaneous Pez dispensers, keepsake ornaments, and more

A collector's dream lineup – from investment-grade graded cards to modern fire and deep superhero inventory. Questions? Call or text Jensen Jeffrey at 260-273-5931.

title: 2002 Bombardier Quest 650
description: One owner, Rotax Carbureted 644 cc, liquid-cooled, four-stroke engine.  All wheel drive with automatic transmission (low, high, reverse, park), 2,500# cable winch, front and rear cargo racks.  Moose 60" detachable snowplow with electric up/down actuator mounted on plow frame and manual angle adjustment.  The acutuator allows for electric up/down while leaving the ATV winch available to use independently.  The package was purchased new from AJ's in Mansfield and has always been serviced there.  Always stored inside - the original seat has no tears or rips.  Gas gauge and odometer haven't worked in quite some time but estimated mileage is 2,000 +/- miles.  The Dirt Devil 6-ply tires are knee deep in tread but show some dry rot crazing between the lugs.  Tires were treated with "Slime" years ago and have always held air.  This is a heavier machine that can push a crazy amount of snow!!  When spring arrives, simply drop the snowplow (5-10 minutes) and hit the trails.  She Runs Great!!

title: Pop Culture Time Capsule: TV Memorabilia & Vintage Toys
description:
Prepare for an unparalleled journey through television history and pop culture as Willow Auction House presents over 400 captivating lots of Pop Culture and TV memorabilia. This auction features selections from two distinguished collections: The Estate of Stewart Berkowitz, known for its exceptional collection of screen-used props from the 1950s, 60s, and 70s. This extraordinary sale offers a rare glimpse into television's golden age, spotlighting an extensive collection of Jackie Gleason and The Honeymooners props, including Gleason's suit, Ralph Kramden's lunchbox, and on-screen apartment furniture. Also from Berkowitz’s discerning eye are an iconic Batman searchlight, a Star Trek Original Series reproduction control center, and a coveted second-season I Dream of Jeannie bottle. Other items include three life-size Marx Brothers display figures, vintage television studio cameras, a Batman Classic TV Series pinball machine and a 1968 Yamaha Catalina motorcycle and trailer.

The sale also includes a thrilling assortment of new-old-stock and graded toys from the legendary collection of Igal Maoz former owner of the famed Children of Paradise toy store on Bleecker Street in New York City, including highly sought-after Star Wars figures, a huge collection of vintage action figures from G.I. Joe to Marvel and DC superheroes, vintage Japanese toys, classic monsters, rare tin toys, and much more, promising an irresistible treasure trove for enthusiasts of all generations.

title: Recreational Retreat for Sale in Giles County
description:
Recreational Retreat for Sale in Giles County
Check out this property that is surrounded by the Jefferson National Forest. Property offering privacy, scenic views, and abundant wildlife. Owned by the same family since 1961, it features miles of recently cleared and maintained four-wheeler trails, 10 ponds (several spring-fed), multiple potential cabin sites, and the option to live fully off the grid.
Sitting at nearly 3,000 feet in elevation, the property provides views and a true connection to nature. Despite its elevation, the land lives up to the name Flat Top Mountain, with an impressive amount of level to gently rolling land. Improvements include several existing buildings, a generator shed, and an original log cabin that is over 100 years old.
Recreational opportunities are endless, and the property is conveniently located near Dismal Falls and extensive Jefferson National Forest trail systems. Don’t miss out!
Online Auction Ending Wednesday, March 25th, at 4 PM
TBD Flat Top Rd., Pearisburg, VA 24134
CONTACT: Auctioneer Matt Gallimore at 540-239-2585 or email Gallimore.Matt@gmail.com. You can also contact Kathy Hight with Mabry & Cox Realtors at 540-616-6789 or email kathy@mabrycox.com
DIRECTIONS: From Dublin, N. Rt. 100; left on Rt. 42; right on Rt.606 Wilderness Road; right on Rt. 629 Flat Top Road; keep straight until you see a silver gate (see pictures for reference)
BIDDER PACK: Download Bidder Pack for complete information. Bidder Pack has maps, complete terms and conditions, sample contracts, tax cards, and restrictions. Some of the plats in Bidder Pack may have notations and boundaries created by Auctioneer for illustration purposes, however unchanged surveys are in Bidder Pack as well.
SOFT CLOSE: If a bid is received within the last 2 minutes of the auction or after 4 PM, the auction close time will automatically extend 2 minutes to allow other bidders an opportunity to competitively bid prior to the auction closing. This feature eliminates “snipers” and encourages fair and impartial bidding from all participants.
BUYER’S PREMIUM – TEN PERCENT (10%): Buyer’s Premium shall be added to the final online bid, which will determine the Total Contract Sales Price. Bidders hereby acknowledge and understand that the Buyer’s Premium will be added to the winning bid to create the Total Contract Sales Price for which they are obligated to pay for the property. Example: (winning online bid $100,000 + 10% buyer’s premium = total purchase price of $110,000). Buyer’s Premium is not due until closing.
PRE-AUCTION SALES: As an agent for the Seller, the Auctioneer must present any and all bona fide written offers to the Seller, which may be created outside of the online bidding platform. Therefore, all properties are subject to pre-auction sales. Pre-auction offers must meet all off the auction terms and conditions and must be submitted to the Auctioneer on the Auction Real Estate Sales Contract, along with the required earnest deposit. Properly submitted offers will be presented to the Seller in a timely manner. Seller may accept or reject such offer at their sole and absolute discretion. When a pre-auction offer is submitted, all properly registered online bidders will be notified that “an offer” has been submitted and on which specific property, however the amount of the offer shall remain confidential. All pre-auction offers must allow a minimum of 24 hours for seller’s acceptance. A Referral Fee of 2% of the high bid is offered to a cooperating VA State Licensed Real Estate Salesperson/Broker on any pre-auction offers that are properly completed and submitted on the appropriate forms provided. Download Bidder Pack for Online Bidder’s Agreement, maps, sample contracts, and property information.
TERMS AND CONDITIONS: 10% Buyer’s Premium will be added to final high bid to arrive at contract price. Earnest Money Deposit of $20,000. Close on or before May 11th, 2026. Subject to seller confirmation. Property sold as is, where is with any and all faults. Buyer is expected to complete their own due diligence before placing a bid. The Auctioneer is allowed to bid on behalf of the Seller up to, but not beyond the Seller’s reserve price. Auction starts ending at 4 PM on March 25th, 2026. Please note that during the last two minutes of bidding, if a new bid is placed, the clock is automatically extended an additional two minutes. Be sure to register to bid early to ensure you receive notifications regarding any changes to the Auction, Bidder Packet and/or Pre-Auction offers received. Download Bidder Information Pack for complete terms and conditions, purchase contract and maps.

title: 10740 W ST RD 32 Parker City, IN 47368
description:
This home sells with a guaranteed minimum starting bid of $70,000. Bidding ends March 25, 2026 @ 6:00 P.M.  

Discover comfortable, single-level living in this classic ranch-style home offering approximately 960 square feet of well-maintained space. Built in 1963, this three-bedroom, one-bath residence sits on a generous 0.57-acre lot, providing ample room to enjoy the outdoors.

The home features aluminum siding, a composition shingle roof, and a crawlspace foundation. Inside, you’ll find well-kept finishes that reflect the home’s mid-century character. Heating is provided by an electric system, and while there is no central air conditioning, the home’s manageable size and crawl space makes climate control easy to customize.

A detached 2-car garage sits toward the rear of the property, adding valuable storage or workshop potential. The large yard—enhanced by mature shade trees—offers opportunities for gardening, recreation, or future improvements.

Located along State Road 32, the property provides convenient access to local amenities and is within close proximity to quality public schools.

title: 160± Acres in 3 Parcels near Kingman, Arizona
description:
Online Land Auction – 160± Acres in 3 Parcels
Fort Rock Ranches | Near Kingman, Arizona
Welcome to this Online-Only Land Auction featuring approximately 160± acres offered in three separate parcels in Mohave County, Arizona. Buyers may purchase one parcel, multiple parcels, or the entire 160± acres.This Northern Arizona property borders Arizona State Trust land to the north and west, providing exceptional privacy, open views, and long-term surrounding open space. 
The terrain is a mix of generally flat areas and gentle slopes, complemented by scenic rock formations along the northern boundary. The property is well suited for recreation, hunting, off-grid living, seasonal retreat use, or long-term land investment. 
There is no HOA, and annual property taxes are very low. Utilities are not currently installed. Septic would be required for residential use. Water may be hauled or supplied via a private well (buyers should conduct their own due diligence regarding well installation and water availability). Access is via dirt roads, and 4x4 is recommended due to the remote location.
This is an online-only auction conducted through the HiBid platform. Bidding is open to the public, and competitive bidding will determine the final purchase price. Each parcel will be offered individually. Please review all auction terms and conditions prior to placing bids.Bidders must register online and agree to the auction terms. A Buyer’s Premium will be added to the high bid to determine the total purchase price. A non-refundable earnest money deposit will be required from the winning bidder, with closing to occur within the stated timeframe. Property is sold AS-IS, WHERE-IS, with no warranties expressed or implied. Buyers are responsible for verifying all information prior to bidding.
If you are seeking privacy, wide-open space, and long-term potential in Northern Arizona, this is your opportunity to bid and buy at auction.
For property information and maps, visit: AZLandAuction.com
For Additional Information Contact:John PayneUnited Country Real Estate – Arizona Property & Auction , 480-422-6800.  Stewart LarsenThe Larsen Company Real Estate & Auctions, 480-861-2530.

Happy Bidding!

title: 1,280 Acres Near Oakwood, OK - Fred Holsapple Estate
description:
Presenting the Fred Holsapple Estate - a rare opportunity to own a truly versatile piece of Oklahoma countryside, offered at auction. Whether you're a beginning producer ready to put down roots, an established operator looking to expand, an investor seeking strong potential, or a buyer dreaming of recreational land or the perfect homesite, this property checks every box.

While being cross-fenced, the land offers flexibility for grazing on native grass acres or the option to incorporate cropland grazing with ease. Abundant water resources - including solar wells, electric wells, and multiple runoff ponds - provide reliability and support for livestock and land management.

Adding to its appeal, wind turbines located on Tracts 1 and 6 create an additional income stream, blending agricultural use with long-term investment potential. With most tracts having been idle for the past few years, buyers will appreciate the freedom and clean slate this property offers for future plans, improvements, and customized management.

Don't miss your chance to own part of this exceptional estate. The auction will be held Thursday, March 26, at 10:00 a.m. at the Oakwood Skating Rink.

title: Country Home & Acreage for Sale off The Blue Ridge Parkway
description:
Check out this beautifully designed 3 bed, 2.5 bath home offering +/- 3,182 sq ft of finished living space with a layout that provides both comfort and flexibility. Home is situated on +/- 88 acres of mostly open land with a barn, spring fed pond, views, and seclusion.
The main level provides an abundant space for both everyday living and entertainment. It has +/- 2,372 sq ft and it includes a spacious great room, separate living room, formal dining room, a half-bath, kitchen, and an Eat In Kitchen—offering multiple gathering areas to suit any occasion. You can also find the master bedroom and a full bath on this level. There is also a fireplace in the great room. Enjoy the large composite deck—perfect for relaxing, grilling, or entertaining guests.
The basement level includes 2 bedrooms, 1 full bath, 2 storage areas, and direct access to the attached two-bay garage. This level offers approximately +/- 810 sq ft of finished space and +/- 2,292 sq ft of unfinished space, providing storage or future expansion potential. You will also find a wood stove down in the basement for another source of heat.
With the brick exterior and classic shingle roof create timeless curb appeal. Throughout the home, you’ll find a blend of carpet, hardwood, and tile flooring. Served by well and septic systems, along with a heat pump and a concrete foundation.
The beautiful brick home sits on +/- 88.2 acres, the property features a barn, beautiful open fields (currently used as corn fields), several outbuildings, and stunning views of the Blue Ridge Mountains. A portion of the property is fenced and also features a spring fed pond! This property is right off the Blue Ridge Parkway, making it great access to nature. The property’s access is between Milepost 163 and Milepost 164.
Property is accessed by private drive Soapstone Hill Road off the Blue Ridge Parkway. According to Floyd County GIS Map, VDOT (Virginia Department of Transportation) map, and survey this road continues between the two tracts offered to Roberson Mill Road and Hope Road. The road is overgrown and unmaintained. According to VDOT, the road has been discontinued. There is also a 50' Right of Way that goes along edge of adjacent tract located at 2332 Woods Gap Road. This is a Right of Way only. No road has been built. If New Owner wants access from Woods Gap Road, they would need to build road within Right of Way. Refer to maps in Bidder Pack.
Property is being sold with a 1-year Home Warranty, Standard Plan, which covers heating & air, plumbing, electrical, appliances, and much more. Click on link below for more details.
Online Auction Ends Thursday, March 26th at 4 PM
494 Soapstone Hill Rd., Floyd, VA 24091
PREVIEW DATES: Friday, March 6th at 4 PM and Saturday, March 14th at 10 AM
CONTACT: Auctioneer Matt Gallimore at 540-239-2585 or email Gallimore.Matt@gmail.com. You can also contact Realtor Levi Hylton at 540-392-9626 or email LeviHyltonRealtor@gmail.com
DIRECTIONS: From the stoplight in Floyd VA – take 221 N towards Roanoke, turn right onto Barberry Rd., at the stop sign turn right on to to the Blue Ridge Parkway, continue for 2 miles and then turn right on Soapstone Hill Rd and property will be at the end of the road.
BIDDER PACK: Download Bidder Pack for complete information. Bidder Pack has maps, complete terms and conditions, sample contracts, tax cards, and restrictions. Some of the plats in Bidder Pack may have notations and boundaries created by Auctioneer for illustration purposes, however unchanged surveys are in Bidder Pack as well.
SOFT CLOSE: If a bid is received within the last 2 minutes of the auction or after 4 PM, the auction close time will automatically extend 2 minutes to allow other bidders an opportunity to competitively bid prior to the auction closing. This feature eliminates “snipers” and encourages fair and impartial bidding from all participants.
BUYER’S PREMIUM – TEN PERCENT (10%): Buyer’s Premium shall be added to the final online bid, which will determine the Total Contract Sales Price. Bidders hereby acknowledge and understand that the Buyer’s Premium will be added to the winning bid to create the Total Contract Sales Price for which they are obligated to pay for the property. Example: (winning online bid $100,000 + 10% buyer’s premium = total purchase price of $110,000). Buyer’s Premium is not due until closing.
PRE-AUCTION SALES: As an agent for the Seller, the Auctioneer must present any and all bona fide written offers to the Seller, which may be created outside of the online bidding platform. Therefore, all properties are subject to pre-auction sales. Pre-auction offers must meet all off the auction terms and conditions and must be submitted to the Auctioneer on the Auction Real Estate Sales Contract, along with the required earnest deposit. Properly submitted offers will be presented to the Seller in a timely manner. Seller may accept or reject such offer at their sole and absolute discretion. When a pre-auction offer is submitted, all properly registered online bidders will be notified that “an offer” has been submitted and on which specific property, however the amount of the offer shall remain confidential. All pre-auction offers must allow a minimum of 24 hours for seller’s acceptance. A Referral Fee of 2% of the high bid is offered to a cooperating VA State Licensed Real Estate Salesperson/Broker on any pre-auction offers that are properly completed and submitted on the appropriate forms provided. Download Bidder Pack for Online Bidder’s Agreement, maps, sample contracts, and property information.
TERMS & CONDITIONS: 10% Buyer’s Premium will be added to final high bid to arrive at contract price. Earnest Money Deposit of $10,000 is required on the day of sale. Close on or before May 11th, 2026. Subject to seller confirmation. Property sold as is, where is with any and all faults. Buyer is expected to complete their own due diligence before placing a bid. The Auctioneer is allowed to bid on behalf of the Seller up to, but not beyond the Seller’s reserve price. Auction starts ending at 4 PM on March 26th, 2026. Please note that during the last two minutes of bidding, if a new bid is placed, the clock is automatically extended an additional two minutes. Be sure to register to bid early to ensure you receive notifications regarding any changes to the Auction, Bidder Packet and/or Pre-Auction offers received. Download Bidder Information Pack for complete terms and conditions, purchase contract and maps at BidBlueRidge.com.

title: Ham Radios, Early Antique Radios & More!
description:
Schulman Auction & Realty is pleased to present an outstanding online-only auction featuring ham radio equipment, early antique radios, telegraphy instruments, and radio test equipment.
This sale opens with an exceptional group of early wireless and antique radios, including a remarkable lineup of Atwater Kent breadboard sets (Models 1, 2, 4, 7, 9, 10 and 12), along with important early radio equipment by Western Electric, including the 25-B amplifier with 205D tubes, 4B receiver, 7A amplifier and 2A current supply.
Collectors will also find highly desirable pieces such as the Sparton 558 Peach Mirrored “Sled” radio designed by Walter Dorwin Teague, Catalin radios by Emerson, FADA and Bendix, and other decorative and collectible sets.
Amateur radio operators will appreciate the wide selection of equipment including Alpha, Yaesu, Icom, Kenwood, Elecraft, Ten-Tec, and SGC gear, along with QRP transceivers, amplifiers, antenna tuners, station accessories and test equipment.
A particularly strong section of the auction features telegraph and Morse equipment, including Vibroplex bugs, Bunnell keys, McElroy stream keys, military J-38 keys, railroad sounders and modern precision paddles by Begali, Bencher, Brown Brothers, GHD and others.
Whether you collect early radio history, operate CW, or build amateur radio stations, this auction offers a wide variety of desirable equipment spanning more than a century of wireless technology.

Thank you for looking,
David, WD0ERU & Charles, KF0COM

Rare On-the-Water Opportunity on the Missouri River – Mandan, ND
March 28th, 2026

Location: 4010 Southbay Dr. SE Mandan, ND
11:00 AM

Don’t miss this opportunity to own Dockside LLC, one of only three on-the-water gas stations on the river — and the only fuel station on the Mandan side.
Strategically located next to:
A busy public boat ramp
A fully occupied marina
Steady seasonal boat traffic
A built-in, repeat customer base
This is a true turnkey waterfront business generating revenue from fuel sales and boat rentals, with strong upside potential for off-season expansion.

All movable supplies included

Lease Information
Building & property lease: $833.33/month
Contract good until 2027
Tanks Lease to own (29 Months left $1084 a month)

Auctioneer Notes

Dockside is a well-established and profitable business in the Bismarck / Mandan area and one of only three businesses selling gas and snacks directly on the Missouri River.  Operating successfully for over 7 years, Dockside has built a strong customer base and steady river traffic. The current owners are willing to assist with transition to help the new buyer prepare for a successful 2026 season.  Don’t miss this opportunity to own a proven, turnkey riverfront business.


Business Overview

Dockside LLC is one of only three on-the-water gas stations on the river — and the only one located on the Mandan side of the Mandan / Bismarck area.  Strategically positioned next to a busy public boat ramp and a fully occupied marina, Dockside benefits from steady seasonal boat traffic and a built-in repeat customer base.  Operating successfully and profitably for over 7 years, this is a rare opportunity to step into a proven waterfront business.

The current owners are willing to assist with transition and help position the new buyer for a strong 2026 season.

title: On-Site Forest City
description:
On-Site Forest City — The Collection of Donald Butler

We are honored to present The Collection of Donald Butler, an exceptional on-site estate auction in Forest City, North Carolina. This sale features a thoughtfully curated selection of beloved personal possessions from the estate of Donald Baxter “Don” Butler (1938–2025), whose lifelong appreciation for quality, craftsmanship, and history is reflected throughout this distinguished collection.Please read all information prior to bidding. 

Payment
New customers with invoices exceeding $1,000 must pay by cash or Zelle (ACH) $2.00 surcharge on all Zelle payments. Established clients may pay by cash, approved check, credit card, or debit card.Credit and debit cards on file will be automatically charged within three (3) hours of the auction ending.Invoices over $1,000 must be paid through LiveAuctioneers invoicing, Zelle, or cash. We do not accept credit card payments over the phone due to chargeback fraud.

Important
We strongly recommend previewing all items prior to purchase.All mechanical items are sold as-is, where-is, with no warranties or guarantees. Please request additional photos or information before bidding if needed.

Pick-Up Information
Pick-up dates are March 28 through April 1, from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM.Items not picked up by April 1 at 4:00 PM will be transported to our facility in Weaverville, NC.If we must transport your item, a surcharge will apply:$30 per standard item$50 per large item (scooters, motorcycles, single-lot bicycles, etc.)If you need a vehicle, tractor, or other large item picked up after April 1, arrangements must be made prior to the auction. We may not be able to accommodate late requests.

Shipping
If you require shipping for a small item or group of small items, a $25 per item surcharge will apply to transport the item(s) from the auction site to our facility and deliver them to the shipper.Not all items are eligible for shipping.Shipping costs charged by the shipping company are separate and the responsibility of the buyer.Group LotsAny large group lots not picked up by Monday, April 1 will be considered abandoned.Abandoned items will be surrendered and no refunds will be issued.

title: WV Glassworks Fenton Spring Fest Special Fenton Glass Auct
description:
Get ready for and unforgettable auction experiance!
This is a specialty glass auction held by West Virginia Glassworks and will close out this years Fenton Spring Fest. This auction will consist of a wonderful collection of Fenton Glassware as well as some specialty glass pieces.
We suggest you place your highest bid prior to the auction starting because we move very fast through the lots and you may not get time to increase your bid before the lot closes. 
This is an auction as described as "public auction" under WV Code. All bidding increments, bidding amounts, bidding styles, time limits are strictly at the discretion of the auctioneer. Also "Prebidding Increments" may change during the LIVE sale; MEANING THEY WILL NOT FOLLOW THE PREBIDDING INCREMENTAL BIDDING CHART.

ATTENTION ALL ONLINE BIDDERS: THIS IS A SIMULCAST AUCTION MEANING THAT IT IS BOTH ONLINE AND PERSON AT THE SAME TIME. IN THE EVENT OF A TIE BID ONLINE THE BID WILL GO TO THE EARLIEST PLACED BID, BUT THAT DOES NOT APPLY ONCE THE LIVE AUCTION STARTS IN REGARDS TO FLOOR BIDDERS. IF THE AUCTIONEER TAKES A BID ON THE FLOOR FOR YOUR MAX BID AMOUNT BEFORE IT IS REACHED THE BID WILL GO TO THE FLOOR. ALL ONLINE BIDDERS MUST PAY VIA THE CC ON FILE UNLESS PRIOR ARRANGEMENTS ARE MADE WITH AUCTIONEER. 

SHIPPING IS AVAILABLE ON ALL ITEMS. THERE WILL BE A FEE FOR HANDLING AND PACKAGING MATERIALS. BUYER PAYS SHIPPING ON ALL ITEMS. We are expierenced glass shippers and we do all packaging in house and use Pirate Ship to save you the most money possible!

"Oak Summit is the Premier Auction Company of the South Branch Valley of West Virginia"
Online Bidding Available via hibid.com 15%  buyers premium if bought online. 10%  buyers premium if purchased in-person in-house at time of auction. There will be a 10% buyers premium for absentee or phone bids. We will be offering 5% off if you pay with cash/check.This will only be offered to in house bidders only.
Terms of sale are cash or check with proper ID, Debit and Credit Cards will be accepted with a 5% Processing Fee. There will be a 10% buyers premium on all absentee or phone bids. If the auctioneer approves you to pay via Paypal (MUST BE APPROVED BEFORE AUCTION BEGINS there will a 5% fee for processing. ALL ONLINE BIDDERS MUST PAY VIA THE CC ON FILE UNLESS PRIOR ARRANGEMENTS ARE MADE WITH AUCTIONEER. 
Titled items will be released day of sale, but if payment is made by check titles will be held and mailed once the check clears. Announcements on sale day precedence over any advertising. Licensed and bonded in favor of the State of WV Lic #2151

The Buyer also must provide the Seller with photos of the box, packing materials, and broken item(s). Buyer must report any damages to seller within 24 hours of item being received at distributor. Buyer is also aware that they must purchase additional insurance from distributor for overseas shipment. title: UsedTwo Auction 82
description:
Welcome to UsedTwo Auctions. Preview images of catalog items will become available as upcoming auctions are processed. The complete Auction Catalog, including all preview images, will be available by Monday evening prior to the commencement of the auction.

In-person preview appointments are available Tuesday through Thursday from 10:00 a.m. to 2:00 p.m. during the active auction period. Previews will be conducted at our Frederick warehouse, situated at 7313 Grove Road, Warehouse Unit G, Frederick, MD 21704. Please contact 301-685-5677 to confirm appointment availability.

Auctions operate from Tuesday to Tuesday. Item collection is scheduled for the Thursday following auction closure, from 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM. In the event that Thursday pickup is not feasible, please contact us via email at team@usedtwo.com prior to auction close to arrange an alternative pickup time. Items remaining uncollected by the close of business on the following Thursday, for which no rescheduling arrangement has been communicated, may be forfeited. UsedTwo disclaims liability for any items not retrieved within the designated pickup timeframe.

Shipping/Pickup Information

We do not provide shipping services. However, clients may utilize our local UPS shipping service for item pickup, packing, and shipping.

Important Notice:

Items must be scheduled for pickup no later than 9 AM on the Friday following the auction's conclusion. UPS will collect items on the Friday following the auction's close, provided all required documentation has been submitted.

Documentation Requirements:
A photograph of the signed invoice.
A photograph of the driver's license with matching address.
Both documents must be emailed prior to the release of items for shipping to team@usedtwo.com and The UPS Store at store5681@theupsstore.com. Failure to provide the required documentation will result in the items not being released, invoices not being refunded, and forfeiture of the items. Packing services are provided exclusively by UPS. Clients are required to utilize our designated local UPS Store for pickup, packing, and shipping services. Prepaid shipping labels are not accepted. A copy of the invoice and a shipping address matching the address on file with Hibid must be submitted to The UPS Store before items can be released for shipping and packing. For out-of-state clients and those purchasing jewelry, gold, silver, or other items at our discretion, additional requirements apply: 1. A copy of the invoice and a shipping address matching the Hibid address must be submitted to The UPS Store. 2. A valid driver's license (photo or scan) with an address matching the Hibid address must be provided. 3. The paid invoice (attached to the winning bidder email) must be signed, photographed/scanned, and emailed back to us. Release of items to UPS pickup is contingent upon receipt of all required documents.

To schedule a pickup time and date, please email team@usedtwo.com, specifying that items should be collected from UsedTwo Auctions, 7313 Grove Road Unit G, Frederick MD 21704. Contact information for The UPS Store is as follows:

Your Team at The UPS Store
5257 Buckeystown Pike
Frederick, MD 21704
Phone: 301-620-8825 Fax: 301-620-8821
Website: https://www.theupsstore.com/5681

Subscribe to our Newsletter and follow us on Facebook to become a UsedTwo Auction Insider, gaining access to pre-auction Sneak Peeks and more.

Please ensure UsedTwo communications are not directed to your spam folder. We aim to promptly notify you of winning bids.

For any questions or concerns, please email team@usedtwo.com.
